results.title: WellUpgradeable can be upgraded by anyone
results.reasoning: Yes. The audit explicitly reports “Missing Access Control on upgradeTo/upgradeToAndCall Allows Unauthorized Upgrades” in WellUpgradeable, noting that upgradeTo/upgradeToAndCall and _authorizeUpgrade lack onlyOwner/msg.sender checks, enabling anyone to upgrade to any Aquifer-registered implementation. This is the same issue as the description: missing access control on the UUPS upgrade path in WellUpgradeable. It involves the same code path (upgradeTo/upgradeToAndCall → _authorizeUpgrade) and the same fix (add onlyOwner to _authorizeUpgrade). Although the audit notes other bugs that may currently brick upgrades, it still identifies the underlying access control flaw; adding onlyOwner would fix the described vulnerability.

results.title: Incorrectly assigned decimal1 parameter upon decoding
results.reasoning: Yes. The audit explicitly identifies the same bug in Stable2.decodeWellData: the second conditional incorrectly checks decimal0 == 0 instead of decimal1 == 0, so decimal1 never defaults to 18 when zero. It cites the same function and logic path, notes the same downstream impact on scaling and core functions (calcLpTokenSupply, calcReserve, calcRate, calcReserveAtRatioSwap, calcReserveAtRatioLiquidity), and would be fixed by changing the check to decimal1 == 0—the same fix as in the description. Thus, fixing the report’s issue would fix the described vulnerability.
